title: Quantum average correlations and complementarity relations via metric-adjusted skew information
authors: Xiaoyu Ma, Qing-Hua Zhang, Cong Xu
abstract: We investigate quantum average correlations and complementarity relations based on metric-adjusted skew information. Several natural averaging procedures are considered, including complete families of mutually unbiased bases, all orthonormal bases, operator orthonormal bases, and twirling channels induced by the unitary group. All these approaches lead to the same closed expression, which identifies the resulting average correlation as an intrinsic quantity independent of the averaging scheme. By defining measures of wave and particle features via metric-adjusted skew information, we establish complementarity relations among wave and particle features, quantum entropy, and average correlation. These results provide a unified framework for investigating quantum average correlations and complementarity relations in terms of metric-adjusted skew information.
summary: This paper introduces a unified framework to study quantum correlations and complementarity using metric-adjusted skew information. It demonstrates that quantum average correlation is an intrinsic property that remains invariant regardless of whether one averages over different bases or uses twirling channels. Furthermore, the authors establish new complementarity relations that link wave-like features, particle-like features, and quantum entropy. These results provide a consistent way to quantify the interplay between different quantum properties in bipartite systems.
main_problem: The paper seeks to establish a unified framework for quantifying quantum average correlations and complementarity relations using metric-adjusted skew information.
main_result: The authors prove that quantum average correlation is an intrinsic quantity independent of the averaging scheme (MUBs, Haar measure, etc.) and derive new wave-particle-entropy complementarity relations.
method: The study utilizes the mathematical framework of metric-adjusted skew information based on the Morozova-Chentsov function and employs various averaging procedures including twirling channels and Mutually Unbiased Bases.
model_or_system: The study focuses on bipartite quantum systems (rho_AB) acting on a composite Hilbert space (H_A tensor H_B) and single-party systems.
key_observables: Metric-adjusted skew information, average coherence, quantum average correlation, wave feature (W_c), particle feature (P_c), and quantum f-entropy.
important_parameters: Hilbert space dimensions (d_A, d_B), purity of the environment, and the Morozova-Chentsov function (f).
main_assumptions: The equivalence of averaging schemes assumes the existence of a complete family of MUBs (requiring d_A to be a prime power) and assumes the metric is regular (f(0) > 0).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the framework of metric-adjusted skew information, demonstrates the equivalence of various averaging schemes for correlation, establishes new complementarity relations for single and bipartite systems, and provides formal mathematical proofs in the appendices.
why_interesting: This work provides a rigorous, unified mathematical foundation for understanding the fundamental trade-offs between coherence, uncertainty, and correlation, which is central to the study of open quantum systems and quantum information processing.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
